prompt: Open tender for a school exposes the employer to:

options.text: Wide bidder pool with higher bid review burden and potential non-compliant low bids

explanation: Open tender maximises competition but increases evaluation workload.

distractorExplanations.a: Open tender increases volume of bids to evaluate.
distractorExplanations.c: Open tender requires rigorous compliance filtering.
distractorExplanations.d: Open tender still must comply with regulations.

prompt: NEC secondary options for X clauses include:

options.text: Additional conditions such as delay damages, performance bonds, and sectional completion

explanation: NEC secondary options (X/Y/Z) tailor commercial risk allocation.

distractorExplanations.a: Secondary options supplement core NEC clauses.
distractorExplanations.b: X clauses address substantive commercial risk.
distractorExplanations.d: Secondary options are selected per project.

prompt: Employer delays issuing possession under JCT. Contractor remedy includes:

options.text: No remedy because contractor waits silently
Extension of time and/or loss and expense per contract if employer delay is relevant event

explanation: Employer possession delay may trigger JCT EOT and loss/expense claims.

distractorExplanations.a: Remedies follow contract notice and relevant events.
distractorExplanations.b: JCT provides EOT/LAE mechanisms for employer delay.

prompt: Best value procurement in public sector evaluates:

options.text: Price and quality criteria weighted per published methodology — not price alone

explanation: Public best value procurement uses weighted price/quality evaluation.

distractorExplanations.b: Most public procedures allow quality/social value weighting.

prompt: JCT Design & Build contractor's duty to complete design includes:

options.text: No obligation if employer had concept architect
Developing employer's requirements to deliver compliant completed works
Unlimited redesign of employer spatial requirements

explanation: D&B contractors complete design to satisfy employer's requirements.

distractorExplanations.a: D&B contractor completes design to ER standard.
distractorExplanations.c: Design completion is integral to D&B obligation.
distractorExplanations.d: Design must meet ERs, not override employer requirements.

prompt: A PCSA before D&B construction contract allows:

options.text: Bypass of all procurement rules automatically
Early contractor involvement for design development, programming, and tender pricing inputs
Guaranteed construction award without competition if PCSA signed

explanation: PCSA enables early contractor input before main D&B contract.

distractorExplanations.a: PCSA is pre-construction; construction needs main contract.
distractorExplanations.b: PCSA must comply with applicable procurement.
distractorExplanations.d: Award terms depend on procurement strategy and regulations.

prompt: Tender evaluation matrix weighting 60% price, 40% quality requires:

options.text: Documented scoring of quality sub-criteria and arithmetically combined weighted result

explanation: Weighted tender evaluation requires transparent documented scoring.

distractorExplanations.c: Evaluation criteria should be fixed pre-tender.

prompt: NEC Clause 63 assesses compensation events using:

options.text: Projected defined cost of event with disallowable cost rules

explanation: NEC CE valuation under Clause 63 uses defined cost principles.

distractorExplanations.b: Clause 63 uses defined cost assessment rules.
distractorExplanations.c: NEC has its own CE valuation mechanism.
distractorExplanations.d: CE assessment requires defined cost evidence.

prompt: Contractor design portion under JCT SBC with Contractor's Designed Portion requires:

options.text: No insurance because employer designs all
Identification of CDP works in contract documents with appropriate design liability insurance
Full D&B without stating CDP scope
Employer indemnifies all design errors unconditionally

explanation: JCT CDP requires defined scope and design liability provisions.

distractorExplanations.a: CDP allocates design liability requiring insurance.
distractorExplanations.c: CDP scope must be identified in contract.
distractorExplanations.d: Liability allocation follows contract and CDP scope.

prompt: Framework pricing refresh after 3 years should:

options.text: Lock original rates forever regardless of market
Follow framework rules — mini-competition or indexation/rebenchmark per terms
Use only one supplier without competition ever

explanation: Framework rate refresh follows contractual refresh or mini-competition rules.

prompt: Bonds and guarantees in procurement typically include:

options.text: No security on any public project
Guarantee of unlimited profit to contractor
Bid bonds, performance bonds, and parent company guarantees per contract risk profile

explanation: Procurement security packages align with contract risk and employer policy.

distractorExplanations.b: Bonds are formal financial security instruments.
distractorExplanations.c: Bonds secure performance, not contractor profit.

prompt: JCT fluctuation provisions (optional) address:

options.text: Material/labour price movement via formula indices during contract period

explanation: JCT optional fluctuation provisions pass formula-based price movement.

distractorExplanations.b: Fluctuations address input cost movement, not profit.
distractorExplanations.c: Fluctuations relate to price indices, not defects timing.

prompt: E-tendering platform use in UK public procurement ensures:

options.text: Audit trail, deadline enforcement, and equal information to all bidders

explanation: E-tendering supports fair transparent auditable procurement.

prompt: NEC early contractor involvement under Option X22 supports:

options.text: Collaborative design development with target cost evolution before main construction

explanation: NEC X22 ECI enables collaborative target cost development.

distractorExplanations.a: ECI evolves target cost; CEs still apply.
distractorExplanations.d: Project Manager remains; ECI adds collaboration.

prompt: Letter of intent risk for employer includes:

options.text: No risk — LOI equals full JCT contract
Automatic transfer of all design liability to employer always
Limited scope, unclear terms, and payment/dispute exposure if full contract delayed

explanation: LOIs carry scope and contractual certainty risks requiring careful drafting.

distractorExplanations.b: LOI terms define scope and risk — often limited.
distractorExplanations.c: LOI does not guarantee final pricing outcomes.

prompt: Contractor claims EOT under JCT for exceptionally adverse weather. Must demonstrate:

options.text: Relevant event, causal delay to completion, and notice within contractual period
Only financial loss without delay analysis

explanation: JCT EOT claims need relevant event, causation, and compliant notice.

distractorExplanations.b: Weather EOT requires exceptional threshold and causation.
distractorExplanations.d: EOT addresses time; prolongation costs are separate.

prompt: Procurement fraud red flag in tendering is:

options.text: Collusive pricing patterns, identical errors across bids, or confidential info leaks

explanation: Bid collusion indicators include abnormal patterns and information leaks.

distractorExplanations.a: Close pricing alone is not necessarily collusion.

prompt: FIDIC force majeure/exceptional events clause effect may include:

options.text: Extension of time, cost relief, or termination rights per sub-clause and event type
Contractor always bears all force majeure cost

explanation: FIDIC exceptional events trigger defined contractual remedies.

distractorExplanations.a: Relief depends on FIDIC clause and event circumstances.
distractorExplanations.b: FIDIC addresses exceptional events with defined remedies.

prompt: JCT practical completion is generally evidenced by:

options.text: Certificate of practical completion when works substantially complete per contract definition

explanation: JCT practical completion triggers defects period and retention release stages.

distractorExplanations.b: Practical completion is end of works substantially complete.

prompt: Standstill period after public contract award notification allows:

options.text: Challenging bidders to seek remedies before contract signature per procurement remedies
Immediate contract signature same day without wait

explanation: Procurement standstill protects challenger rights before contract execution.

distractorExplanations.b: Standstill is a defined minimum period.

prompt: NEC compensation event for client-specified material unavailability requires:

options.text: Notification, quotation assessment, and adjustment to programme/prices per NEC process

explanation: NEC CE procedures address client-risk events with quotation assessment.

distractorExplanations.a: Client-risk events may be compensation events.
distractorExplanations.c: NEC provides CE mechanism before termination.
distractorExplanations.d: CEs assess time and cost effects.

prompt: Domestic subcontract on JCT project — back-to-back means:

options.text: Subcontract terms aligned to main contract obligations flowing down appropriately

explanation: Back-to-back subcontracts align obligations with main contract risk allocation.

distractorExplanations.b: Alignment prevents gaps in risk flow-down.
distractorExplanations.d: Contractor procures subs; terms align to main contract.

prompt: APC Level 3 contract practice evidence should show:

options.text: Delegating all contract decisions to lawyer without QS input
Advising client/employer on form selection, risk allocation, and administering key provisions on live project

explanation: Level 3 contract practice is demonstrated through form selection and administration advice.

distractorExplanations.b: Contract practice includes active administration advice.
distractorExplanations.c: QS provides commercial contract advice alongside legal.

prompt: Early supply chain engagement in procurement improves:

options.text: Buildability input, realistic pricing, and risk identification before tender

explanation: Early supply chain input improves price realism and risk visibility.

distractorExplanations.c: Early engagement reduces risk but does not eliminate defects.
distractorExplanations.d: H&S remains integral to procurement and design.

prompt: JCT sectional completion allows:

options.text: Only one completion date for all phases always
Phased completion certificates with sectional liquidated damages and partial possession
Automatic waiver of all defects liability

explanation: JCT sectional completion supports phased handover and damages.

distractorExplanations.c: Retention can still apply per section.
